entities.description: pcie2 0 is a technology interface that requires specific design considerations for signal and power requirements
ac     is a product used for ac coupling in pcie2 0 interfaces  with specific recommendations for packaging and performance
0201   is a product packaging type recommended for ac     to achieve lower esr and esl
clkreqn is a technology signal used in pcie2 0 interfaces that must be connected to a functional pin and cannot be replaced by gpio
waken is a technology signal used in pcie2 0 interfaces that must be connected to a functional pin and cannot be replaced by gpio
m0 is a technology configuration option that must be selected for clkreqn and waken signals in pcie2 0 interfaces
m1 is a technology configuration option that must be selected for clkreqn and waken signals in pcie2 0 interfaces
m2 is a technology configuration option that must be selected for clkreqn and waken signals in pcie2 0 interfaces
perstn is a technology signal used in pcie2 0 interfaces that can be connected to a functional pin or gpio  but must be in the same group as clkreqn and waken if using a functional pin
prsnt is a technology signal used in pcie2 0 interfaces for add incard insertion detection  which can be connected to gpio
sata usb30 is a technology that cannot be used when pcie2 0 functionality is active  as their corresponding modules are not available
rk3588 is a product used for pcie30      with specific data line and control signal connection requirements
txp txn is a technology signal used in pcie interfaces for data transmission  which must be connected in a cross manner for pcie30
rxp rxn is a technology signal used in pcie interfaces for data reception  which must be connected in a cross manner for pcie30
refclkp refclkn is a technology signal used in pcie interfaces for reference clock  which must meet the same source clock requirements for pcie30 phy and slot peripherals
phy is a technology component used in pcie interfaces that requires the refclkp n signals to be sourced from the same clock generator
slot is a technology component used in pcie interfaces that requires specific design considerations for signal and power requirements
pcie30 is a technology interface that requires specific design considerations for signal and power requirements  with different pin configurations compared to pcie2 0

relations.description: ac     is used in pcie2 0 interfaces for ac coupling  with specific recommendations for packaging and performance
clkreqn is a signal used in pcie2 0 interfaces that must be connected to a functional pin and cannot be replaced by gpio
waken is a signal used in pcie2 0 interfaces that must be connected to a functional pin and cannot be replaced by gpio
clkreqn must be selected with  m0 or  m1 or  m2  and cannot be mixed with different configurations
waken must be selected with  m0 or  m1 or  m2  and cannot be mixed with different configurations
perstn must be in the same group as clkreqn and waken if using a functional pin
perstn must be in the same group as clkreqn and waken if using a functional pin
prsnt can be connected to gpio for add incard insertion detection
sata usb30 cannot be used when pcie2 0 functionality is active  as their corresponding modules are not available
rk3588 uses txp txn signals for data transmission in pcie30      with cross connections
rk3588 uses rxp rxn signals for data reception in pcie30      with cross connections
refclkp refclkn must meet the same source clock requirements for pcie30 phy and slot peripherals
phy and slot peripherals require the refclkp n signals to be sourced from the same clock generator
pcie30 uses txp txn signals for data transmission  which must be connected in a cross manner for
pcie30 uses rxp rxn signals for data reception  which must be connected in a cross manner for
pcie30 uses refclkp refclkn signals for reference clock  which must meet the same source clock requirements for phy and slot peripherals
